commit.message:
fix: use type cleanup instead of <code> tags for MDX table compatibility

<code> HTML tags don't work inside MDX table cells either. Instead,
strip <ArrayBufferLike> in cleanupGenericTypes() and use backtick code
spans with brace-only escaping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: escape <ArrayBufferLike> in generated MDX files

Fix the already-generated MDX files that contain raw <ArrayBufferLike>
which the MDX parser interprets as unclosed JSX tags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: escape angle brackets in generated SDK docs to prevent MDX parse errors

TypeScript generic types like <ArrayBufferLike> were being interpreted as
JSX tags by the MDX parser, breaking both the link checker and Vercel
deployment. Use <code> HTML tags with </> escaping for inline types
in tables, and escape angle brackets in description text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Ask AI widget toggle and layout stability (#2872)

commit.message:
fix: use @2xl container breakpoint for AI tools banner columns

The @md container breakpoint (448px) was too small — the banner was
still wider than that with the sidebar open. Bump to @2xl (672px) so
columns only go side-by-side when the banner genuinely has enough room.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use container queries for AI tools banner two-column layout

Media queries respond to viewport width, not the actual container width.
When the Decimal push-sidebar is open the viewport stays wide but the
content area shrinks, keeping the two-column layout even when there is
not enough room. Switch to @container / @md: so the columns stack based
on the banner's own width.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Ask AI widget toggle and layout stability

- Add overflow-x:hidden and max-width transition to body so the Decimal
  push-sidebar animates smoothly without horizontal scroll
- Replace stale module-level widgetOpen flag with DOM query
  (`.decimal-widget-sidebar.open`) so toggle stays in sync when the
  widget is closed by the user directly
- Bump AI tools banner two-column breakpoint from sm to md so it stacks
  gracefully when the sidebar squeezes the content area

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use overflow-y-auto on search scroll container for default links

The wrapper used overflow-hidden which clipped the default links list
on small mobile viewports. Change to overflow-y-auto so both the
default links and search results branches are scrollable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: redesign AIToolsBanner with warm gradient, two-column layout

Replaces the grid-pattern background with a warm orange gradient,
adds two-column Skills+CLI layout, compact spacing, and proper
async clipboard handling matching the PromptBanner style.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: target search results with wrapper class instead of data-empty

The previous CSS used div[data-empty] to target the SearchDialogList,
which is a fumadocs internal attribute. Replace with a controlled
wrapper div (.search-scroll-container) around the results area so the
flex-shrink and max-height overrides apply regardless of list state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable mobile scrolling in search dialog

The search results list was unscrollable on mobile because the dialog
had no max-height constraint and the inner list used a fixed max-h of
460px that exceeded the viewport when the keyboard was open. Constrain
the dialog to 100dvh on mobile with flex layout so the list shrinks to
fit the available space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:
tree 7de6a7bd08c19bf8fd7b6ee825e179c469930a9e

author Sushmitha Mallesh <sushdec6@gmail.com> 1773091508 -0700
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1773091508 -0700

docs: Update toolkits and API data (#2859)

commit.message: docs: update toolkits and API data

fix(e2e): expect [] from toolkits search on no results

CI builds a fresh Docker image (no layer cache), so the binary includes
the ui.output('[]') change. Update the assertion to match.

fix(e2e): revert search no-results assertion to expect empty stdout

The e2e tests run against a cached Docker image tagged by CLI version.
The image is only rebuilt on version bumps, so the ui.output('[]')
source change isn't reflected until the next release. Revert the e2e
assertion to match the currently built binary's behavior.

The source code change (outputting [] on no results) is correct and
will be verified after the next Docker image rebuild.

fix(cli): warn when --connected filter is bypassed and set exit code on errors

Two fixes for toolkits list/search:

1. When --connected is requested but session data is unavailable (API
   failure), warn the user that the filter could not be applied instead
   of silently returning unfiltered results.

2. Set process.exitCode = 1 in the catchAll error handlers so CI/scripts
   can detect API failures via exit code. The friendly error message and
   [] output are preserved for clean UX.

perf(cli): parallelize catalog fetch and session creation in toolkits list

Run the catalog API call and Tool Router session creation concurrently
using Effect.all with unbounded concurrency. The session toolkits call
still runs sequentially after session creation (it needs the session ID).
This saves one network round trip (~100-500ms) when a user ID is available.

fix(cli): align bash rc file selection with install.sh fallback

The install command now checks .bashrc then .bash_profile for the bash
shell, picking the first existing file (or defaulting to .bashrc).
This matches the install.sh fallback behavior and prevents the two
paths from writing to different files.

fix(cli): use atomic write for rc file updates in install command

Write to a temp file then rename, so a crash or Ctrl+C mid-write
cannot leave the user's shell config file truncated or corrupted.

fix(install): validate COMPOSIO_GITHUB_* env vars to prevent supply-chain attacks

The install script accepts COMPOSIO_GITHUB_URL, COMPOSIO_GITHUB_OWNER,
and COMPOSIO_GITHUB_REPO from the environment to construct the download
URL. A malicious override could redirect the download to an attacker-
controlled server, bypassing checksum verification (since checksums.txt
is fetched from the same source).

Fix:
- Require COMPOSIO_GITHUB_URL to start with https://
- Require COMPOSIO_GITHUB_OWNER and COMPOSIO_GITHUB_REPO to match
  safe identifier patterns (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots)

fix(cli): prevent shell injection via COMPOSIO_INSTALL_DIR in rc files

The install command writes COMPOSIO_INSTALL_DIR into shell rc files
(.zshrc, .bashrc, config.fish). Previously the value was unquoted,
allowing shell metacharacters (;, `, $, |, etc.) to inject arbitrary
commands that execute every time the user opens a terminal.

Fix:
- Quote the install directory value in all path blocks (bash, zsh, fish)
- Add isUnsafePath validation that rejects paths with metacharacters
- Quote the value in the manual setup note for unknown shells
- Quote the fish and zsh fallback paths in install.sh
- Add test for unsafe path rejection

fix(cli): add debug logging to silent catchAll handlers in install command

Replace silent error swallowing with Effect.logDebug in the rc file read
and directory creation error handlers, matching the pattern used elsewhere
in the codebase (e.g., toolkits.list.cmd.ts session fetch error handler).

fix(cli): output empty JSON array on toolkits search with no results

Align toolkits search with toolkits list: both now emit '[]' to stdout
when no results are found, so piped consumers always get valid JSON.

test(cli): add test for COMPOSIO_INSTALL_DIR default fallback

Verify that when COMPOSIO_INSTALL_DIR is unset, the install command
defaults to ~/.composio for the PATH block written to rc files.

refactor(cli): extract toolkits table column widths into named constant

Replace inline magic numbers in formatToolkitsTable with a TOOLKITS_TABLE
constant, matching the convention used in logs-cmd/format.ts and
triggers/format.ts. Also adds truncation to name/slug/version columns.

fix(cli): apply --connected filter client-side after catalog+session merge

The --connected flag was only passed to the session API, but after
unifying the output the catalog API drives the result set and doesn't
support is_connected. Apply the filter client-side on the merged data.

fix(cli): update toolkits list unit test for unified table format

The unified table now always shows the Connected column (with "-" when
no session data is available), so the test assertion that the column
should be absent without --user-id is no longer valid.

fix(cli): unify toolkits list/search output into a single stable schema

The `toolkits list` command had two separate code paths producing
different JSON schemas depending on whether a user_id was resolved
(session format vs legacy format). This caused e2e test failures when
the test environment had a test_user_id configured, silently switching
to the session format which lacked catalog fields like latest_version.

Consolidate into a single path: always fetch from the catalog API
(which provides tools_count, triggers_count, latest_version), then
optionally enrich with session connection data when a user_id is
available. The output schema is now stable regardless of context.

fix(cli): gracefully handle API errors in toolkits list/search

When the API returns an error (e.g. 401), the list and search commands
now catch the error, log it via ui.log.error (visible in interactive
mode, suppressed in piped mode), and output '[]' to stdout so piped
consumers always receive valid JSON.

This follows the same pattern already used in toolkits.info.cmd.ts.

fix(e2e): use parseJsonStdout in CLI toolkits tests

Replace bare JSON.parse(sanitizeOutput(result.stdout)) with the new
parseJsonStdout helper so that CLI errors (e.g. 401) produce clear
messages instead of cryptic 'SyntaxError: JSON Parse error: Unexpected EOF'.

fix(e2e): add parseJsonStdout helper for clear error messages

When the CLI errors out, stdout is empty and JSON.parse("") throws a
cryptic SyntaxError. The new helper surfaces the actual exit code and
stderr so the root cause is immediately visible in test output.

fix(cli): exit with non-zero code on unhandled errors

The global error handler in bin.ts caught errors for pretty-printing
but then succeeded, causing the teardown function to exit with code 0.
Scripts piping CLI output could not detect failures via exit code.
fix(cli): update test expectation to include 'install' subcommand

fix(install): show CLI output on success and always display post-install guidance

- Replace 2>/dev/null with a temp file capture so the CLI's TerminalUI
  output (written to stderr) is shown when the command succeeds, but
  suppressed on failure to avoid noise.
- Move the "To get started" guidance block outside the if/else so it
  always displays regardless of which code path ran.

fix(install): use exact line matching for shell config marker detection

grep -qF does substring matching, so the PATH marker "# Composio CLI"
would falsely match "# Composio CLI completions". Switch to grep -qxF
to require full-line matches, consistent with the TS fileContains logic.
Merge branch 'next' into feat/cli-install-improvements

docs: add PromptBanner with AI coding prompts to quickstart (#2858)

commit.message:
fix(docs): address Claude review — aria-hidden on SVG, mobile wrap

- Add aria-hidden="true" to decorative sparkle SVG
- Add flex-wrap on mobile, flex-nowrap on sm+ to prevent overflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test(docs): add static tests for quickstart PromptBanner

- Validate all 6 prompts exist with required sections
- Verify deprecated section includes code examples
- Guard against markdown headings in prompts (TOC pollution)
- Ensure mdxToCleanMarkdown strips PromptBanner from .md output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(docs): improve PromptBanner robustness

- Await clipboard.writeText and only show success on fulfilled promise
- Clean up setTimeout on unmount and debounce rapid clicks via timerRef
- Extract pure DOM helpers (detectLang, getTabLabel, extractSteps) outside component
- Eliminate duplicate querySelector('code') call in extractSteps loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rewrite prompt code extraction to fix Radix tab leak

- Walk up from pre to step boundary only (not past it) to find tab panels
- Match tab panel to trigger by index position in sibling panels/tabs
- Improve language detection (data-language, data-lang, content heuristics)
- Broader step title selector (h2-h4, class patterns)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: fix Radix tab ID leak in copied prompt — use aria-labelledby to find trigger text

The tab panel's data-value contains Radix internal IDs like
"radix _r_1k_ trigger python". Instead, look up the tab trigger
element via aria-labelledby to get the clean label ("Python").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

readme_html:
Composio SDK

Skills that evolve for your Agents

🌐 Website • 📚 Documentation

This repository contains the official Software Development Kits (SDKs) for Composio, providing seamless integration capabilities for Python and Typescript Agentic Frameworks and Libraries.

Getting Started

TypeScript SDK Installation

# Using npm
npm install @composio/core

# Using yarn
yarn add @composio/core

# Using pnpm
pnpm add @composio/core

Quick start:

import { Composio } from '@composio/core';
// Initialize the SDK
const composio = new Composio({
  // apiKey: 'your-api-key',
});

Simple Agent with OpenAI Agents

npm install @composio/openai-agents @openai/agents

import { Composio } from '@composio/core';
import { OpenAIAgentsProvider } from '@composio/openai-agents';
import { Agent, run } from '@openai/agents';

const composio = new Composio({
  provider: new OpenAIAgentsProvider(),
});

const userId = 'user@acme.org';

const tools = await composio.tools.get(userId, {
  toolkits: ['HACKERNEWS'],
});

const agent = new Agent({
  name: 'Hackernews assistant',
  tools: tools,
});

const result = await run(agent, 'What is the latest hackernews post about?');

console.log(JSON.stringify(result.finalOutput, null, 2));
// will return the response from the agent with data from HACKERNEWS API.

Python SDK Installation

# Using pip
pip install composio

# Using poetry
poetry add composio

Quick start:

from composio import Composio

composio = Composio(
  # api_key="your-api-key",
)

Simple Agent with OpenAI Agents

pip install composio_openai_agents openai-agents

import asyncio
from agents import Agent, Runner
from composio import Composio
from composio_openai_agents import OpenAIAgentsProvider

# Initialize Composio client with OpenAI Agents Provider
composio = Composio(provider=OpenAIAgentsProvider())

user_id = "user@acme.org"
tools = composio.tools.get(user_id=user_id, toolkits=["HACKERNEWS"])

# Create an agent with the tools
agent = Agent(
    name="Hackernews Agent",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant.",
    tools=tools,
)

# Run the agent
async def main():
    result = await Runner.run(
        starting_agent=agent,
        input="What's the latest Hackernews post about?",
    )
    print(result.final_output)

asyncio.run(main())
# will return the response from the agent with data from HACKERNEWS API.

For more detailed usage instructions and examples, please refer to each SDK's specific documentation.

Open API Specification

To update the OpenAPI specifications used for generating SDK documentation:

# Pull the latest API specifications from the backend
pnpm api:pull

This command pulls the OpenAPI specification from https://backend.composio.dev/api/v3/openapi.json and updates the local API documentation files.

This is pulled automatically with build step.

Available SDKs

TypeScript SDK (/ts)

The TypeScript SDK provides a modern, type-safe way to interact with Composio's services. It's designed for both Node.js and browser environments, offering full TypeScript support with comprehensive type definitions.

For detailed information about the TypeScript SDK, please refer to the TypeScript SDK Documentation.

Python SDK (/python)

The Python SDK offers a Pythonic interface to Composio's services, making it easy to integrate Composio into your Python applications. It supports Python 3.10+ and follows modern Python development practices.

For detailed information about the Python SDK, please refer to the Python SDK Documentation.

Provider Support

The following table shows which AI frameworks and platforms are supported in each SDK:

Provider TypeScript Python
OpenAI ✅ ✅
OpenAI Agents ✅ ✅
Anthropic ✅ ✅
LangChain ✅ ✅
LangGraph ✅* ✅
LlamaIndex ✅ ✅
Vercel AI SDK ✅ ❌
Google Gemini ✅ ✅
Google ADK ❌ ✅
Mastra ✅ ❌
Cloudflare Workers AI ✅ ❌
CrewAI ❌ ✅
AutoGen ❌ ✅

* LangGraph in TypeScript is supported via the @composio/langchain package.

Don't see your provider? Learn how to build a custom provider to integrate with any AI framework.

Packages

Core Packages

Package Version
TypeScript
@composio/core
Python
composio

Provider Packages

Package Version
TypeScript
@composio/openai
@composio/openai-agents
@composio/anthropic
@composio/langchain
@composio/llamaindex
@composio/vercel
@composio/google
@composio/mastra
@composio/cloudflare
Python
composio-openai
composio-openai-agents
composio-anthropic
composio-langchain
composio-langgraph
composio-llamaindex
composio-crewai
composio-autogen
composio-gemini
composio-google
composio-google-adk

Utility Packages

Package Version
@composio/json-schema-to-zod
@composio/ts-builders

if you are looking for the older sdk, you can find them here

Rube

Rube is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server built with Composio. It connects your AI tools to 500+ apps like Gmail, Slack, GitHub, and Notion. Simply install it in your AI client, authenticate once with your apps, and start asking your AI to perform real actions like "Send an email" or "Create a task."

It integrates with major AI clients like Cursor, Claude Desktop, VS Code, Claude Code and any custom MCP‑compatible client. You can switch between these clients and your integrations follow you.

Contributing

We welcome contributions to both SDKs! Please read our contribution guidelines before submitting pull requests.

License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

Support

If you encounter any issues or have questions about the SDKs:

  • Open an issue in this repository
  • Contact our support team
  • Check our documentation
